requirements.location: src/protontricks/cli/main.py — `main()` function (argument parsing section and the `elif args.command:` branch near line 373)
requirements.description: The main CLI is missing a `--cwd-app` argument. Additionally, when `--command` (`-c`) is used, the current implementation unconditionally sets `cwd=str(steam_app.install_path)` when calling `run_command()` (line 385), but the test `test_run_command` requires `command.cwd` to be `None` by default. The `test_run_command_cwd_app` test expects that only when `--cwd-app` is provided should the working directory be set to the game's installation path.
requirements.contract:
1. Add a `--cwd-app` boolean flag (store_true) to the argument parser in `main()`. When provided, the working directory for the shell command should be set to the game's installation path (`steam_app.install_path`). When not provided, `cwd` must not be passed to `run_command()` (or be `None`).
      2. The `--cwd-app` flag should only affect the `-c`/`--command` code path, not the winetricks command path.
      3. Signature contract for the updated call: `run_command(..., cwd=str(steam_app.install_path), shell=True)` only when `args.cwd_app` is True; otherwise `run_command(..., shell=True)` without a `cwd` argument.

requirements.location: src/protontricks/cli/launch.py — `main()` function (argument parsing section near lines 51–98, and the inner_args construction near lines 178–181)
requirements.description:
Two problems exist in `protontricks-launch`:
      (a) The executable path is wrapped in single quotes (`f"'{executable_path}'"`) when building the wine command string (line 179). This causes `command.args.endswith("/test.exe")` to fail because the string ends with `test.exe'` instead of `test.exe`.
      (b) The `--cwd-app` flag is not defined in the `launch.py` argument parser. When a caller passes `--cwd-app`, argparse raises a SystemExit(2) error. The `test_run_executable_passthrough_arguments` test passes `--cwd-app` and expects it to be forwarded to `cli_main` as part of `cli_args`.
requirements.contract:
(a) The executable path in the wine command string must not be wrapped in single quotes. The path may be shell-quoted in a way that preserves the `endswith("/test.exe")` or `endswith("test.exe")` assertion (i.e., the final portion must end with the bare filename, not with a trailing quote character). Using `shlex.quote` is acceptable only if it does not add trailing quote characters that break the assertion; alternatively, the path can be used without quoting.
      (b) Add a `--cwd-app` boolean flag (store_true) to `launch.py`'s argument parser. When present, include `"--cwd-app"` in the `cli_args` list passed to `cli_main`. The expected forwarded `cli_args[0:7]` for the `test_run_executable_passthrough_arguments` call is: `["-v", "--no-runtime", "--no-bwrap", "--no-background-wineserver", "--no-term", "--cwd-app", "-c"]`.

requirements.description: After successfully parsing a VDF file (no `SyntaxError`), the function directly accesses `compat_tools["compatibilitytools"]` without guarding against a missing key. If the VDF data is structurally valid (no parse error) but semantically incomplete (e.g., the top-level key is misspelled or absent), a `KeyError: 'compatibilitytools'` is raised and propagates to the caller, aborting the entire app discovery process. The two failing test cases use VDF content with either an empty dict `{}` or a wrong key name (`"incompatibilitytools"`).
requirements.contract: After `vdf_data` is successfully parsed, the case-insensitive lookup for `"compatibilitytools"` in the lowercased `vdf_data` dict must be wrapped in a guard. If the key is absent, log a warning (similar to the existing corrupted-file warning) and `continue` to the next VDF file rather than raising a `KeyError`. The same guard should apply to the subsequent `"compat_tools"` key lookup. The warning message should indicate the file path and suggest reinstallation. After this change, a corrupted or empty VDF file must not cause an exception to propagate; instead it is silently skipped and the function continues processing remaining VDF files.

requirements.location:
src/protontricks/steam.py — `find_steam_installations()` function (around lines 391–405);
      src/protontricks/gui.py — `select_steam_installation()` function (around lines 247–252)
requirements.description:
Two related problems with Snap Steam support:
      (a) `find_steam_installations()` handles the Flatpak path (`.var/app/com.valvesoftware.Steam/data/Steam`) specially — ensuring `steam_root == steam_path`. However, the Snap Steam path (`snap/steam/common/.local/share/Steam`) is not handled; it falls into the COMMON_STEAM_DIRS loop where `steam_root` defaults to `~/.steam/root`, causing both paths to diverge and the test's `next(...)` iterator to raise `StopIteration` because the entry with matching `steam_path` has the wrong `steam_root`.
      (b) `select_steam_installation()` only detects Flatpak installations (checking `endswith("/com.valvesoftware.Steam/.local/share/Steam")`). Snap installations are labeled "Native" instead of "Snap".
requirements.contract:
(a) In `find_steam_installations()`, add a Snap Steam path (`~/.snap/steam/common/.local/share/Steam` or the equivalent relative path `snap/steam/common/.local/share/Steam` under `Path.home()`) handled analogously to the Flatpak path: if the directory has a steamapps subdirectory, add a candidate where both `steam_path` and `steam_root` point to the same Snap directory (not `~/.steam/root`).
      (b) In `select_steam_installation()`, add Snap detection: if `steam_path` contains `snap/steam/common/` (or an equivalent indicator), assign the label `"Snap"` instead of `"Native"` or `"Flatpak"`. The priority of label checks should be: Flatpak first, then Snap, then Native.
      Both changes must be consistent: a path recognized as Snap by `find_steam_installations()` must also be labeled "Snap" by `select_steam_installation()`.

requirements.location: src/protontricks/cli/main.py — `main()` function: (a) the no-args early-return block around lines 171–173; (b) the `do_gui` code path around lines 276–294
requirements.description:
Two problems in the main CLI's GUI handling:
      (a) When `protontricks` is invoked with no arguments, the function detects `not do_command and not do_list_apps and not do_gui and not do_winetricks` and calls `parser.print_help()` and returns immediately. The test `test_run_no_args` expects the command to default to GUI mode, showing "No game was selected" when no game is chosen.
      (b) In the `do_gui` branch (around line 276–294), `run_command()` is called without first checking `proton_app.is_proton_ready`. If the Proton installation is incomplete (the `dist`/`files` directory is missing), `proton_app.proton_dist_path` returns `None`, causing a `TypeError` when `run_command()` tries to construct paths like `proton_app.proton_dist_path / "lib64" / "wine"`. The test `test_run_gui_proton_incomplete` expects the error message "Proton installation is incomplete" to be returned.
requirements.contract:
(a) When no arguments are provided (all of `do_command`, `do_list_apps`, `do_gui`, `do_winetricks` are False), the function must set `do_gui = True` and proceed with the GUI flow rather than printing help and returning. All other behaviors of the GUI flow remain unchanged.
      (b) In the `do_gui` branch, after `find_proton_app()` returns a non-None `proton_app` (line ~277–281) and before calling `run_command()`, add the same `is_proton_ready` guard that already exists in the winetricks/command flow (lines 338–343): if `not proton_app.is_proton_ready`, call `exit_("Proton installation is incomplete. Have you launched a Steam app using this Proton version at least once to finish the installation?")`.
